title: Route experiments: missing experiment_id/variant_id attribution on streaming requests (worse on retries) causing gaps in Console results
preview:
Issue Summary
- Customer (Acme AI, route experiments beta) reports that experiment results in Console are inconsistent when using streaming responses. Specifically, p95/p99 latency and cost/token charts show a large percentage of traffic under “Unknown / unattributed” despite the route being under an active experiment.
- Customer verified they are receiving variant headers intermittently on streaming responses.

Customer Impact
- Console experiment dashboards appear incomplete/incorrect (variant comparison not trustworthy).
- Guardrail evaluation confidence reduced because unattributed traffic is excluded from per-variant aggregations.
- Customer is holding ramp from 10% -> 50% until attribution is reliable.

Environment
- Redwood Hosted API via Dedicated capacity.
- Regions: primarily us-east; customer also observed similar behavior after failover to eu-west.
- Endpoints: /v1/chat/completions with streaming enabled.
- Client: Redwood Python SDK + custom retry wrapper (retries on network disconnect / 5xx / upstream timeout).

Expected
- For every request routed under an experiment, experiment_id + variant_id should be present:
  - In response headers (for debugging) where applicable.
  - In request context so OTel spans + metrics emit {route_id, experiment_id, variant_id} consistently.
  - Persisted across streaming lifecycle and across retry paths (including internal re-attempts).

Actual
- Non-streaming requests show near-100% attribution.
- Streaming requests show missing experiment_id/variant_id in:
  - OTel spans for a subset of requests.
  - Aggregated metrics (rollups) feeding Console results.
- Missing rate increases when a retry occurs (disconnect mid-stream or gateway retry).

Steps to Reproduce (internal)
1) Enable a route experiment on a chat route (A=baseline model, B=variant model).
2) Send streaming chat completions with a forced intermittent disconnect (tcp reset or client cancel) to trigger retry logic.
3) Compare:
   - Gateway/router logs: request_id -> selected variant.
   - OTel traces: check for experiment_id/variant_id attributes on root span and streaming child spans.
   - Metrics rollups: attributed vs unattributed counts for the same time window.

Initial Triage Notes (Support + Telemetry)
- Hypothesis A: experiment_id/variant_id set on initial request context but lost when stream is handed off to async writer / span context changes.
- Hypothesis B: retry code path creates a new request context without copying experiment assignment fields.
- Hypothesis C: sampling / cardinality protections dropping tags (less likely; customer observes missing even in traces, not just metrics).

What we checked
- Console Results API confirms high “attribution drop rate” for streaming routes only.
- Router debug endpoint (internal) for same stickiness key shows stable assignment (determinism OK).
- Correlated unattributed spans with “streaming retry” logs; strong correlation.

Workaround (shared with customer)
- Temporary: disable client-side retries on mid-stream disconnects OR retry with a new request_id but include an explicit, stable stickiness key header to reduce confusing partial aggregates.
- Not a true fix; attribution still missing on some internal retry flows.

Engineering Escalation
- Owner: Telemetry/Tracing (Caleb Johnson) with Router context plumbing review (Vikram Singh / Jordan Lee as needed).
- Requested: verify request context propagation across streaming + retry, ensure experiment attribution fields are attached to:
  - Root request span
  - Streaming chunk spans (if present)
  - Any retry attempt spans

Resolution
- Fix implemented in github:redwood/pr-28622-fix-attribution-missing-on-streaming-retries.

text:
Issue Summary
- Customer (Acme AI, route experiments beta) reports that experiment results in Console are inconsistent when using streaming responses. Specifically, p95/p99 latency and cost/token charts show a large percentage of traffic under “Unknown / unattributed” despite the route being under an active experiment.
- Customer verified they are receiving variant headers intermittently on streaming responses.

Customer Impact
- Console experiment dashboards appear incomplete/incorrect (variant comparison not trustworthy).
- Guardrail evaluation confidence reduced because unattributed traffic is excluded from per-variant aggregations.
- Customer is holding ramp from 10% -> 50% until attribution is reliable.

Environment
- Redwood Hosted API via Dedicated capacity.
- Regions: primarily us-east; customer also observed similar behavior after failover to eu-west.
- Endpoints: /v1/chat/completions with streaming enabled.
- Client: Redwood Python SDK + custom retry wrapper (retries on network disconnect / 5xx / upstream timeout).

Expected
- For every request routed under an experiment, experiment_id + variant_id should be present:
  - In response headers (for debugging) where applicable.
  - In request context so OTel spans + metrics emit {route_id, experiment_id, variant_id} consistently.
  - Persisted across streaming lifecycle and across retry paths (including internal re-attempts).

Actual
- Non-streaming requests show near-100% attribution.
- Streaming requests show missing experiment_id/variant_id in:
  - OTel spans for a subset of requests.
  - Aggregated metrics (rollups) feeding Console results.
- Missing rate increases when a retry occurs (disconnect mid-stream or gateway retry).

Steps to Reproduce (internal)
1) Enable a route experiment on a chat route (A=baseline model, B=variant model).
2) Send streaming chat completions with a forced intermittent disconnect (tcp reset or client cancel) to trigger retry logic.
3) Compare:
   - Gateway/router logs: request_id -> selected variant.
   - OTel traces: check for experiment_id/variant_id attributes on root span and streaming child spans.
   - Metrics rollups: attributed vs unattributed counts for the same time window.

Initial Triage Notes (Support + Telemetry)
- Hypothesis A: experiment_id/variant_id set on initial request context but lost when stream is handed off to async writer / span context changes.
- Hypothesis B: retry code path creates a new request context without copying experiment assignment fields.
- Hypothesis C: sampling / cardinality protections dropping tags (less likely; customer observes missing even in traces, not just metrics).

What we checked
- Console Results API confirms high “attribution drop rate” for streaming routes only.
- Router debug endpoint (internal) for same stickiness key shows stable assignment (determinism OK).
- Correlated unattributed spans with “streaming retry” logs; strong correlation.

Workaround (shared with customer)
- Temporary: disable client-side retries on mid-stream disconnects OR retry with a new request_id but include an explicit, stable stickiness key header to reduce confusing partial aggregates.
- Not a true fix; attribution still missing on some internal retry flows.

Engineering Escalation
- Owner: Telemetry/Tracing (Caleb Johnson) with Router context plumbing review (Vikram Singh / Jordan Lee as needed).
- Requested: verify request context propagation across streaming + retry, ensure experiment attribution fields are attached to:
  - Root request span
  - Streaming chunk spans (if present)
  - Any retry attempt spans

Resolution
- Fix implemented in github:redwood/pr-28622-fix-attribution-missing-on-streaming-retries.
  - Summary (from PR): ensure experiment dimensions persist across retry paths for streaming requests; copy experiment attribution fields into retried request context and ensure span attributes set before stream writer takes over.
- Rollout:
  - Deployed to staging: 2025-03-03
  - Deployed to prod us-east: 2025-03-05
  - Deployed to prod eu-west: 2025-03-06

Validation
- Post-deploy: attribution drop rate on Acme AI route experiments reduced from ~18–25% (streaming) to <1%.
- Customer confirmed variant headers now present consistently on streaming responses; Console results updated within the expected aggregation window.

Follow-ups
- Add regression test for streaming + retry attribution in ENG-9031 style E2E suite (suggested to Console/Platform).
- Ensure docs call out that variant headers may be omitted on certain error responses, but attribution should still be present in telemetry.
